step_by_step_reasoning: We identify the main components from the whiteboard: Users connect to Route 53, which route requests to CloudFront for static content caching or ELB for dynamic EKS-based microservices. The EKS microservices use ElastiCache and RDS for application data storage. To build the data lake, Glue extracts data from RDS, stores it in S3, and loads it into Redshift for analytics.

graph.name: Anima Educacao Monolith Migration to EKS and Data Lake
graph.notes: Migration of a legacy monolithic educational system to an EKS microservices-based architecture integrated with a serverless data lake.

nodes.name: Students
nodes.notes: Students logging into the educational portal.

nodes.name: CloudFront
nodes.notes: Caches static content to reduce latency for users in Brazil.

nodes.name: ELB
nodes.notes: Balances incoming traffic to the EKS cluster.

nodes.name: EKS Cluster
nodes.notes: WORKLOAD_PEEK: Runs about 60 microservices to split up the old monolith.

nodes.name: RDS Databases
nodes.notes: DATA_PEEK: Consists of ~20 databases, with 3 dedicated specifically for enrollment renewal.

nodes.name: ElastiCache
nodes.notes: Caches enrollment data to optimize performance.

nodes.name: AWS Glue
nodes.notes: Performs ETL tasks to load structured data from RDS.

nodes.name: Redshift
nodes.notes: Data warehouse used for business analytics and AI algorithms.

edges.notes: Check cache for fast enrollment renewal data
Glue extracts data from operational RDS databases
Stores transformed data into S3 raw zone
Loads processed data into Redshift analytical warehouse
